description: Pure Go Terminal Backend - Eliminate tcell dependency by implementing terminal I/O with ANSI/VT100 escape sequences

userStories.title: Define Terminal Abstraction Interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a clean Screen interface and supporting types so that main.go can be migrated from tcell to the new pure Go backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create term/ subdirectory with screen.go defining Screen interface: Init() error, Fini(), Size() (int, int), PollEvent() Event, PostEvent(Event), Clear(), SetContent(x, y int, ch rune, style Style), Show(), ShowCursor(x, y int), Sync()
Define Event interface with a marker method
Define KeyEvent struct with Key() KeyCode, Rune() rune, Modifiers() ModMask methods
Define ResizeEvent struct with Size() (int, int) method
Define Style type with Reverse(bool) Style method and a StyleDefault zero value

Define key code constants for all keys used in main.go: KeyNUL, KeyCtrlA through KeyCtrlZ, KeyCtrlSpace, KeyCtrlUnderscore, KeyEnter, KeyBackspace, KeyBackspace2, KeyEsc, KeyUp, KeyDown, KeyLeft, KeyRight, KeyRune
Define ModNone and ModAlt modifier constants
go build ./term/ succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story defines types only -- no implementation yet. Keep the interface minimal, matching only what goomacs currently uses from tcell.

userStories.title: Raw Mode Terminal Setup and Teardown
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to put the terminal into raw mode on startup and restore it on exit so that the editor can receive individual key presses and control screen output.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Terminal struct in term/terminal.go that satisfies the Screen interface (stub methods are OK for now, Init/Fini must work)
Init() saves original termios state, sets raw mode using syscall package (no external deps)
Init() sends ANSI sequences to enter alternate screen buffer (\033[?1049h) and hide cursor (\033[?25l)
Fini() restores original termios, exits alternate screen buffer (\033[?1049l), shows cursor (\033[?25h)
Terminal is properly restored even on panic (using defer pattern)
go build ./term/ succeeds with zero external dependencies
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use syscall.SYS_IOCTL with TCGETS/TCSETS for termios on Linux. The syscall package is part of Go stdlib.

userStories.title: Screen Size Query and Resize Detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to query the terminal size and detect resize events so that the editor can adapt its layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Size() method using TIOCGWINSZ ioctl via syscall package
Detect terminal resize via SIGWINCH signal using os/signal.Notify
Resize events are delivered as ResizeEvent through the same event channel as key events
go build ./term/ succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: TIOCGWINSZ constant is 0x5413 on Linux. Use syscall.Syscall with SYS_IOCTL.

userStories.title: Screen Rendering with ANSI Escape Sequences
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to render characters to the screen so that the editor can display buffer content, status line, and messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement cell-based screen buffer ([][]Cell where Cell has rune and Style)
Clear() resets all cells to space with default style
SetContent(x, y, ch, style) sets a cell in the buffer
Show() diffs current buffer vs previous buffer and writes only changed cells using ANSI cursor positioning (\033[row;colH)
Show() applies reverse video style using \033[7m and resets with \033[0m
ShowCursor(x, y) positions the hardware cursor using \033[row;colH and shows it
Sync() forces full screen redraw (marks all cells as dirty)
Output is buffered with bufio.Writer and flushed once per Show() call
go build ./term/ succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ANSI coordinates are 1-based (row 1, col 1 is top-left). Remember to convert from 0-based cell buffer indices.

userStories.title: Keyboard Input Parsing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to read and parse keyboard input so that all existing Emacs keybindings work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: PollEvent() blocks on stdin read and returns parsed Event
Parses single-byte control characters: C-a through C-z (0x01-0x1A), C-SPC/NUL (0x00), Enter (0x0D), Backspace (0x7F), Escape (0x1B), Tab (0x09), C-_ (0x1F)
Parses ANSI escape sequences for arrow keys: \033[A (Up), \033[B (Down), \033[C (Right), \033[D (Left)
Parses Alt+key as Esc followed by character with ~50ms timeout to distinguish bare Esc
Reads multi-byte UTF-8 rune sequences for printable characters
PostEvent(ev) re-queues an event to be returned by the next PollEvent() call
go build ./term/ succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use select with time.After for Esc-prefix timeout. Read from os.Stdin into a byte buffer, then decode. PostEvent can use a buffered channel or queue.

userStories.title: Automated Tests for Terminal Backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the pure Go terminal backend to verify correctness without a real terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Test keyboard input parsing: control characters map to correct KeyCode values
Test keyboard input parsing: ANSI escape sequences parse to correct arrow key events
Test keyboard input parsing: UTF-8 multi-byte sequences parse to correct runes
Test keyboard input parsing: Alt+key sequences produce ModAlt modifier
Test screen buffer: SetContent stores correct cell values, Clear resets all cells
Test screen rendering: cell diff only outputs changed cells
Test style: Reverse(true) and Reverse(false) produce correct Style values
Test PostEvent: re-queued events are returned before stdin events
go test ./term/ passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: For input parsing tests, inject a bytes.Reader or io.Reader instead of os.Stdin. For screen rendering tests, capture output to a bytes.Buffer.

userStories.title: Integrate Pure Go Backend into main.go
userStories.description: As a user, I want goomacs to use the pure Go terminal backend so that it has zero external dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Update main.go to import goomacs/term instead of github.com/gdamore/tcell/v2
Replace all tcell.Key* constants with term.Key* equivalents
Replace all tcell.Style* usage with term.Style* equivalents
Replace tcell.EventKey / tcell.EventResize with term.KeyEvent / term.ResizeEvent
Replace tcell.Screen parameter types in drawBuffer, drawStatusLine, drawMessageLine with term.Screen
Remove github.com/gdamore/tcell/v2 from go.mod and go.sum via go mod tidy
go.mod contains zero require directives after go mod tidy
go build succeeds and produces working binary
go test ./... passes (both buffer tests and term tests)
Manual test: open file, edit text, save (C-x C-s), search (C-s), undo (C-_), mark/region (C-SPC, C-w), quit (C-x C-c)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the final switchover. All term/ package functionality must be working before this story. The main.go changes are mostly mechanical find-and-replace of tcell types/constants with term equivalents.
